rulings.Case Title: RYAN GARMAN et al VS. UNIVERSITY PIPELINE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Tom Unger And Stephanie Losee'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And For Sanctions And Statement Of Items
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, April 10, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANTS TOM UNGER AND STEPHANIE LOSEE'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And For Sanctions And Statement Of Items. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION et al VS. RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Intention To Move For New Trial
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, April 10, 2014, Line 17, PLAINTIFFs MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION, KAROL MEIER and SANDI SELIG-FARNEY's Motion For New Trial. The motion for new trial is denied. Plaintiffs fail to present new facts that warrant a new trial. The plaintiffs lack diligence in presenting the new matter, fail to demonstrate that the Court's order is legally erroneous, and the motion refers to alleged damages from the Nursing Board and The Doctors Company that are not alleged in the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: AMANI GORDON VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, April 10, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. As to the first cause of action, "[w]hen an injury occurs despite a defendant's efforts to provide security or supervision, it is relatively easy to claim that, ipso facto, the security or supervision provided was ineffective. Without more, such claims fail." (Thompson v. Sacramento City Unified School Dist. (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1352, 1370.) Defendant met its burden showing there was security and supervision on campus on the day of the incident. (UMF no. 18.) Plaintiff does not set forth any evidence establishing that there is "more" here. There is no evidence that the particular kind of harm was foreseeable. (See M.W. v. Panama Buena Vista Union School Dist. (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 508, holding that the attack was foreseeable because the school knew students were unsupervised at the time the attack occurred, plaintiff had repeatedly complained to the principal about the defendant mistreating him, and the principal testified that sexual assault among special education students was a concern prior to the assault.) Plaintiff does not set forth any evidence showing that an employee of the school acted negligently. (See Dailey v. Los Angeles Unified Sch. Dist. (1970) 2 Cal.3d 741, holding that the school district could be held vicariously liable because the teacher assigned to supervise the area where the injury occurred sat with his back to the window and failed to notice a crowd of thirty students gathering to watch the fight that caused the harm.) As to the second cause of action, Defendant met its burden showing the stairwell was not a dangerous condition, or alternatively, Defendant did not have actual or constructive knowledge of the dangerous condition. (UMF nos. 16, 17, 20, 21.) Plaintiff has not set forth evidence establishing a dangerous condition. Plaintiff argues that the stairwell was dangerous because of a lack of cameras, failure to warn of the lack of cameras, and failure to monitor the hallway. However, the testimony cited as the evidence of this assertion does not reference any of these alleged dangerous conditions. (Deposition of Amani Gordon, pp. 96:9-12, 98:1-19.) Thus, Plaintiff's arguments about the dangerous condition are unsupported by the evidence. Further, Plaintiff sets forth no evidence showing that Defendant had actual or constructive knowledge of the defect, as required by Govt. Code sec. 835.2. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARLON ESTACIO PAGTAKHAN VS. THE SF EXAMINER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Responses To Special And For Interrogatories And Req. For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, April 10, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANTS THE SF EXAMINER, TODD VOGT, MIKE ALDAX and ARI BURACK'S Motion To Compel Responses To Special And Form Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants¿ Motion to Compel Responses to Special and Form Interrogatories is GRANTED. Plaintiff MARLON ESTACIO PAGTAKHAN shall serve verified responses, without objection, to Defendants¿ Special Interrogatories, Set One and Form Interrogatories, Set One on or before April 30, 2014. Defendants¿ request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ERIN COLLINS; INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHAL OF OTHER VS. THE GAP, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defts To Provide Further Responses To Pltf'S Special Interrogatories (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, April 10, 2014, Line 10, PLAINTIFF ERIN COLLINS' Motion To Compel Defendants To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories (Set One). Denied. Given the Order Granting Preliminary Approval of Class Action Settlement issued on March 24, 2014 in Harmon v. The Gap, Inc. (Riverside Cty. Sup. Ct., case no. RIC 120620) ("Harmon"), at this juncture, Defendants are not required to provide further responses until the conclusion of that settlement. It is clear from the pleadings in both cases that Plaintiff in this action is within the class covered by the pleadings in Harmon and that the class that Plaintiff seeks to represent in this case as well as the alleged wrongs sought to be remedied are essentially the same wrongs as asserted in Harmon. It would be a waste of the parties' resources and of the court's judicial efforts to require further discovery at this point. When the settlement in Harmon is finalized, Plaintiff in this action may either accept the terms of the settlement or opt out of any settlement and proceed in this action seeking to represent herself and/or to represent all other alleged class members who may similarly opt out of the Harmon settlement. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ERIN COLLINS; INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHAL OF OTHER VS. THE GAP, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Motion To Compel Deft To Provide Further Responses To Pltf'S Form Interrogatory-General (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, April 10, 2014, Line 10, PLAINTIFF ERIN COLLINS' Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatory- General (Set One). Denied. Given the Order Granting Preliminary Approval of Class Action Settlement issued on March 24, 2014 in Harmon v. The Gap, Inc. (Riverside Cty. Sup. Ct., case no. RIC 120620) ("Harmon"), at this juncture, Defendants are not required to provide further responses until the conclusion of that settlement. It is clear from the pleadings in both cases that Plaintiff in this action is within the class covered by the pleadings in Harmon and that the class that Plaintiff seeks to represent in this case as well as the alleged wrongs sought to be remedied are essentially the same wrongs as asserted in Harmon. It would be a waste of the parties' resources and of the court's judicial efforts to require further discovery at this point. When the settlement in Harmon is finalized, Plaintiff in this action may either accept the terms of the settlement or opt out of any settlement and proceed in this action seeking to represent herself and/or to represent all other alleged class members who may similarly opt out of the Harmon settlement. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: EVAN C NELSON VS. TUCKER ELLIS, LLP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff For Limited Exemption From Automatic Stay
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, April 10, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFF EVAN NELSON's Motion Of Plaintiff For Limited Exemption From Automatic Stay. Granted. (CCP § 916.) A limited exemption to hear Defendant's special motion to strike, which was originally taken off calendar in light of the automatic stay, will not affect the effectiveness of the appeal pursuant to the factors enumerated in Varian Medical Systems, Inc. v. Delfino (2005) 35 Cal.4th 180, 188-190. Defendant's special motion to strike is re-set for hearing on May 16, 2014 at 9:30am in Department 302. Defendant shall deliver and courtesy copies of the moving papers filed on January 10, 2014 to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 11, 2014 at 4 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Defendant shall serve by mail these same moving papers on Plaintiff no later than this same date. Opposition and reply shall be filed and served in accordance with CCP § 1005(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
